title: Gomphia guatemalensis
taxonomy: Gomphia guatemalensis is classified in the family Ochnaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the polypetalous family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. The Ochnaceae family is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions around the world. Gomphia guatemalensis, as part of this family, is a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Gomphia guatemalensis helps to understand its relationships with other plants and its place in the plant kingdom.

intro: Gomphia guatemalensis is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Ochnaceae and commonly known as "Guatemalan Gomphia" or "Ochna". The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gomphia guatemalensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gomphia guatemalensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and to promote overall well-being.
